behavior_summary: U8P_DRIPS::anim advances the animation frame for a 'drips' item on each animation tick: it reads the current frame, increments it, wraps the value back to 0 if it exceeds 14 (0x0E), triggers a sound effect when the new frame equals 10 (0x0A), and writes the updated frame back to the item.

The sound is played at a specific animation frame using a word taken from the item plus two constant parameters (0x64 and 0x00A3). This appears intended to synchronize an audio cue with a visual frame.
No global flags are read or written by this script; its effects are localized to the item instance passed in.
notable_control_flow: Called as a periodic animation/tick handler (Event 2) for the item instance.
1) Call Item::getFrame(this) to read current frame.
4) If new_frame == 0x0A (10) then call playSFX(item_field, 0x64, 0x00A3) to play a synchronized sound effect.
5) Call Item::setFrame(this, new_frame) to store the updated frame back to the item.
open_questions: Which exact item field is pushed as the first argument to playSFX (a word read via indirect access)?
What do the numeric constants passed to playSFX represent (0x64, 0x00A3) in engine terms — volume, priority, or sound id mapping?
Are the frame thresholds (wrap at 0x0E, trigger at 0x0A) chosen to match specific sprite frames or timing in the visual animation?
How often Event 2 (the U8P_DRIPS::anim tick) is invoked for this item instance in game-time units?

main_function.name: U8P_DRIPS::anim
main_function.purpose: Advance and wrap the item's animation frame on each animation tick and play a sound effect at a designated frame.
entry_conditions: Invoked as the item's animation/tick handler (Event 2) — called periodically by the engine to update the item's animation.

function_map.name: U8P_DRIPS::anim
function_map.role: animation/tick handler for a drips item; reads and writes the item's frame and triggers frame-tied SFX

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: read current animation frame/index from the item instance

function_map.name: U8P_DRIPS::playSFX
function_map.role: play a sound effect (called when new_frame == 0x0A); first arg is a word taken from the item, followed by constants 0x64 and 0x00A3

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: write the computed new animation frame back into the item instance

external_calls.purpose: Retrieve the item's current animation frame before incrementing.
Play a synchronized sound effect when the animation reaches frame 0x0A; first argument appears to be a word read from the item, the other args are constants (0x64, 0x00A3).
Store the new/wrapped animation frame back on the item instance.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_DRIPS::anim advances the animation frame for a 'drips' item on each animation tick: it reads the current frame, increments it, wraps the value back to 0 if it exceeds 14 (0x0E), triggers a sound effect when the new frame equals 10 (0x0A), and writes the updated frame back to the item.
